subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Components
- **Airport board** — Double-sided board representing the approach; one side per airport/scenario.
- **Cockpit board** — Central board with dice placement slots for both players.
- **Airplane standee** — Tracks the plane's position on the approach track.
- **Approach track** — Shows altitude levels the plane must descend through.

#### Dice
- **8 dice total** — 4 orange dice (Captain/Pilot) and 4 blue dice (First Officer/Co-pilot).
- **Standard d6** — Values 1-6 on each die.

#### Tokens & Markers
- **Airplane tokens** — Traffic planes on the approach that must be cleared.
- **Axis marker** — Tracks the plane's balance (tilt left or right).
- **Speed marker** — Tracks the plane's current speed.
- **Landing gear tokens** — 3 tokens to deploy sequentially.
- **Flap tokens** — 2 tokens to deploy sequentially.
- **Brake marker** — Tracks braking power for final approach.
- **Coffee tokens** — Allow re-rolling a die (limited supply).

#### Scenario Components
- **Airport cards** — Different airports with unique challenges and module additions.
- **Module cards** — Add mechanics like wind, ice, kerosene, interns, etc.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Select airport** — Choose an airport card (Montreal for beginners).
2. **Place cockpit board** — Set the cockpit board between both players.
3. **Place approach board** — Set up the approach track beside the cockpit.
4. **Position airplane** — Place the airplane standee at the top of the approach track.
5. **Set axis** — Place the axis marker at the center (balanced) position.
6. **Place traffic** — Place airplane tokens on the approach track as indicated by the airport card.
7. **Landing gear** — Place 3 landing gear tokens in their undeployed positions.
8. **Flaps** — Place 2 flap tokens in their undeployed positions.
9. **Set speed** — Place the speed marker at the starting position.
10. **Coffee tokens** — Place the indicated number of coffee tokens in the supply.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Roles
- **Captain (Pilot)** — Sits on the left; uses orange dice.
- **First Officer (Co-pilot)** — Sits on the right; uses blue dice.
- **Permanent roles** — Players keep the same role for the entire game.

#### Dice
- **4 dice each** — Each player takes their 4 colored dice.
- **Secret rolls** — Dice are rolled behind a screen or hidden from partner.

#### Communication Restriction
- **No discussing dice values** — Players cannot reveal, hint at, or discuss their specific dice values.
- **General strategy allowed** — You may discuss strategy, priorities, and preferences in general terms.
- **Trust-based** — The game relies on reading your partner and making educated guesses.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Both players roll** — Each player secretly rolls their 4 dice behind their screen.
2. **Simultaneously reveal** — Both players reveal all dice at the same time.
3. **Alternating placement** — Starting with the Captain, players alternate placing 1 die at a time on cockpit slots.
4. **Complete the round** — After all 8 dice are placed, resolve the round's effects.
5. **Begin approach** — The plane descends and the next round begins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Roll dice** — Both players secretly roll their 4 dice.
2. **Reveal simultaneously** — Show all dice to each other at the same time.
3. **Place dice alternately** — Starting with the Captain, alternate placing 1 die per turn into cockpit slots.
4. **Resolve round** — After all 8 dice are placed, check: axis balance, speed, approach advancement, traffic clearance.
5. **Advance airplane** — Move the plane down the approach track by the number of spaces equal to the current speed.
6. **Check for failure** — If any failure condition is triggered, the game ends in a loss.
7. **Next round** — If the plane has not landed and has not crashed, begin a new round.

#### Number of Rounds
- **Variable** — Depends on the airport; typically 4-7 rounds before landing.
- **Speed determines descent** — Higher speed means fewer rounds but harder to control.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Cockpit Slots
- **Axis (Captain + Co-pilot)** — Both players place 1 die each on axis. The difference determines tilt direction and magnitude. Goal: keep balanced.
- **Engines (Captain or Co-pilot)** — Place dice to control speed. Higher values = faster descent; lower values = slower.
- **Landing Gear (numbered)** — Deploy landing gear by placing dice meeting minimum value requirements (must deploy in order).
- **Flaps (numbered)** — Deploy flaps by placing dice meeting minimum value requirements (must deploy in order).
- **Radio** — Place a die to clear traffic planes from the approach. Die value must meet or exceed the traffic plane's value.
- **Brakes** — Place a die to add braking power for the final landing.
- **Concentration** — Place a die to gain coffee tokens (re-roll ability).

#### Axis Balance
- **Captain's axis die** — Tilts the plane one direction.
- **Co-pilot's axis die** — Tilts the other direction.
- **Balance check** — If the axis marker goes too far in either direction, the plane crashes.
- **Target** — Keep the axis marker near center.

#### Coffee Tokens
- **Re-roll** — Spend a coffee token to re-roll 1 of your dice before placing it.
- **Limited supply** — Coffee tokens are scarce; use wisely.
- **Earned via concentration** — Place a die on the concentration slot to gain tokens.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Communication Limitation
- **No dice discussion** — The core tension comes from not knowing your partner's dice values.
- **Allowed communication** — General priorities ("I can handle radio this round"), preferences, and strategy.
- **Forbidden communication** — Specific numbers, exact values, or pointing at specific dice.

#### Traffic Management
- **Approach planes** — Traffic airplanes sit on the approach track at specific altitudes.
- **Collision risk** — If your plane descends to a space occupied by traffic, you crash.
- **Clearing traffic** — Use the radio slot to remove traffic planes (die value must meet their requirement).
- **Planning ahead** — Clear traffic before descending to those altitudes.

#### Landing Requirements
- **All gear deployed** — All 3 landing gear tokens must be deployed before landing.
- **All flaps deployed** — Both flap tokens must be deployed before landing.
- **Speed at or below limit** — Speed must not exceed the landing threshold.
- **Axis balanced** — The axis must be at center or within tolerance.
- **Approach clear** — No traffic planes in the landing zone.

#### Modules (Advanced Play)
- **Wind** — Shifts the axis unpredictably between rounds.
- **Ice** — Blocks cockpit slots until cleared.
- **Kerosene** — Adds fuel management; run out and you crash.
- **Interns** — Adds a third die-placement slot type with special rules.
- **Campaign airports** — Progressively harder airports with more modules.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- **Safe landing** — The plane reaches the end of the approach track with all landing requirements met.

#### Loss Conditions
- **Axis crash** — The axis marker reaches the extreme position in either direction.
- **Traffic collision** — The plane descends to a space with uncleared traffic.
- **Overshoot** — The plane's speed is too high to stop at the runway.
- **Incomplete deployment** — Landing gear or flaps are not fully deployed when the plane reaches the runway.
- **Approach overrun** — The plane descends past the end of the approach track.

#### Scoring
- **Binary outcome** — The game is a cooperative win or loss; no point scoring.
- **Campaign progression** — Successfully landing unlocks harder airports and new modules.
- **Difficulty scaling** — Each airport has a difficulty rating from beginner to expert.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Balance the axis first** — Crashing from axis imbalance is the most common loss; prioritize axis dice early.
- **Communicate priorities** — Tell your partner which slots you can or want to fill (without revealing numbers).
- **Start clearing traffic early** — Do not wait until the plane is about to reach traffic planes.
- **Deploy gear and flaps gradually** — Spread deployment across rounds rather than rushing or delaying.
- **Manage speed** — A controlled, moderate speed gives more rounds to set up for landing.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring the axis** — Even one unbalanced round can crash the plane.
- **Descending too fast** — High speed leaves fewer rounds to deploy gear, clear traffic, and set up brakes.
- **Hinting at dice values** — Breaking communication rules undermines the game's core challenge.
- **Saving coffee tokens** — Using a re-roll at the right moment can save the game; do not hoard them.
- **Forgetting landing requirements** — Reaching the runway without gear, flaps, or brakes deployed is an instant loss.
- **Not reading your partner** — Pay attention to which slots they fill and what that implies about their remaining dice.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Which die to place first** — Leading with axis or engine sets expectations for your partner.
- **Speed control** — Fast descent reduces rounds but increases pressure; slow descent gives time but risks running out of approach track.
- **Radio vs other slots** — Clearing traffic is mandatory but competes with gear, flaps, and brakes for dice.
- **When to use coffee** — Re-rolling can fix a bad situation, but tokens are limited.
- **Trusting your partner** — Sometimes you must commit a die to a suboptimal slot, trusting your partner to cover a critical one.
